summary: Full backend + frontend testing of wespeak.ai CRM MVP. All 18 pytest backend tests PASSED (health, auth for 4 seeded roles, register, contacts/companies/deals/projects/activities CRUD, deal stage PATCH, activity toggle, dashboard stats, RBAC for guest/admin, admin user mgmt with self-delete block, AI endpoint returning expected 400). Frontend Playwright script PASSED all critical flows: login page renders, demo-admin tile fills credentials, sign-in navigates to dashboard with KPIs and charts, admin sees nav-users/nav-settings, navigation between all sections works, create contact via modal appears in list, deals board/list view toggle works, add-deal-btn present, EN<->HU language toggle works (Dashboard->Irányítópult), dark/light theme toggle works, guest role sees NO add/edit/delete buttons and NO admin nav.

minor.issue: Endpoint uses get_current_user (any authenticated user can list users). PRD context suggests admin-only is expected; consider tightening to require_role('admin').
No safeguard against demoting the last admin — admin can accidentally demote themselves via PUT (self-delete is blocked but self role change is not).
Cookies use samesite=none;secure=true (required for cross-site https). Works fine in preview, but note this fails on plain http/localhost.

action_items: OPTIONAL: Restrict GET /api/users to admin-only (currently any authenticated user can list users incl. password hashes are not exposed but email/roles are).
OPTIONAL: Prevent admins from demoting themselves via PUT /api/users/{id} (parallel to the existing self-delete protection).
critical_code_review_comments: server.py: startup seed() is idempotent and re-hashes admin password if changed — good.
auth.py: bcrypt with $2b$ hash, JWT via httpOnly cookies with samesite=none/secure — matches playbook.
CORS explicitly restricted to FRONTEND_URL with allow_credentials=True — correct.
All routers correctly use require_write for POST/PUT/DELETE/PATCH which blocks guest role at framework layer.
MongoDB '_id' concern N/A — this app uses PostgreSQL via SQLAlchemy with UUID primary keys.
No brute-force lockout implemented on /api/auth/login. Playbook expects lockout after 5 failed attempts — NOT IMPLEMENTED. Non-blocking for MVP but should be added for security.
test_credentials: admin@wespeak.ai/admin123, manager@wespeak.ai/manager123, user@wespeak.ai/user123, guest@wespeak.ai/guest123 (all seeded and verified working)
seed_data_creation: None created; used existing seed data. All test-created records (TEST_ prefixed contacts/companies/deals/projects/activities/users, plus UI test contact) were deleted after verification.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend uses PostgreSQL + SQLAlchemy (not MongoDB despite MONGO_URL being present in .env). JWT is delivered via httpOnly cookies with samesite=none;secure — you must use requests.Session() to preserve them. AI endpoint POST /api/ai/command returning 400 'OpenRouter API key not configured' is EXPECTED, not a bug. Guest role is blocked via require_write dependency. No brute-force lockout on login. All 18 pytest tests pass in ~7s. All frontend data-testid attributes exist as documented.
